rows.prd_item: WS-1: support >=3 logical workspaces (Employer Work, Personal Business, Personal Life)
WS-2: assign workspace before durable processing
WS-3: maintain global coordination metadata (availability, deadlines, cross-workspace priorities, identity matching)
rows.note: identity-matching/identity-map not called out in any REQ acceptance signal; otherwise covered by GCL boundary + scheduling reqs

rows.prd_item: WS-4: prevent raw Employer Work content from appearing in other workspaces by default
WS-5: support user-approved explicit cross-workspace links (Level-3)
rows.note: REQ-F-005 covers cross-workspace READS through GCL, but no REQ covers creating/approving explicit cross-workspace links (§6.3 Level 3). P1.

rows.prd_item: WS-6: open-source users choose Simple/Professional/Founder/Advanced workspace presets
rows.note: Phase 10 mentions 'sample workspace presets' but no REQ-* covers user-selectable workspace presets. P1.

rows.prd_item: WS-7: pass adversarial leakage suite (0 raw Employer Work in other-workspace outputs)
WS-8: workspace-scoped GBrain retrieval; GCL single Visibility Gate; no direct cross-brain queries
KN-1: GBrain required in V1 as the knowledge engine
KN-2: GBrain provides search, think/synthesis, graph, timelines, schema, health, MCP, Minions, dream-cycle
rows.note: REQ-F-005/REQ-D-001 establish GBrain presence + derived/rebuildable, but NO REQ enumerates the required knowledge-engine capability surface (think/graph/timeline/schema/health/MCP/Minions/dream). P0.

rows.prd_item: KN-3: semantic knowledge in Obsidian-compatible Markdown Git repos
KN-4: KnowledgeWriter is the only direct writer of durable semantic Markdown
KN-5: proposed semantic changes submit a KnowledgeMutationPlan, never write directly
KN-6: every approved semantic change auditable and linked to source evidence
rows.note: audit covered by REQ-D-002; source-evidence linkage implied by KnowledgeMutationPlan.sourceRefs but not an explicit REQ acceptance signal

rows.prd_item: KN-7: human-owned note sections shall not be overwritten by the assistant
rows.note: §20.1 has a 'Human-section preservation' acceptance test but NO REQ-* covers the human-owned-section non-overwrite guarantee. P0.

rows.prd_item: KN-8: assistant-generated sections use explicit start/end markers and stable IDs
rows.note: No REQ-* covers assistant-section markers/stable IDs (prerequisite for KN-7 preservation). P1.

rows.prd_item: KN-9: GBrain performs NO direct Markdown writes (native write-through disabled/intercepted)
KN-10: GBrain retrieval returns >=1 relevant item for 90%+ of benchmark query set
rows.note: measured via EVAL corpora; no standalone functional retrieval-relevance REQ but measurement is covered

rows.prd_item: RT-1: AgentRuntimePort with Hermes and Claude Agent SDK adapters in V1
RT-2: agent jobs specify workspace, capability, context, tool policy, max runtime, max cost, output schema, idempotency key
rows.note: tool policy/schema/idempotency covered; max runtime + max cost fields lack a covering REQ — see COST-1 gap

rows.prd_item: RT-3: agent results validated against schema before side effects
RT-4: Temporal orchestrates cross-system workflows and approval waits
RT-5: GBrain Minions for GBrain-internal jobs only unless explicitly bridged
rows.note: Minions-internal restriction implied by one-writer (REQ-F-006); no dedicated REQ naming Minion job scope

rows.prd_item: RT-6: Hermes cron/Kanban supported but not replacing Temporal as workflow source of truth
RT-7: Hermes autonomous schedulers allowed; all external writes via Tool Gateway, all semantic writes via KnowledgeWriter
MTG-1: ingest Granola meetings; correlate to calendar/workspace/project/attendees/history
MTG-2: create/update one canonical meeting note per meeting
MTG-3: extract decisions, commitments, risks, open questions, follow-ups, project state changes
MTG-4: never infer task owners or due dates; mark TBD or route for clarification
rows.note: No REQ-* covers the no-inference / mark-TBD data-integrity rule for unstated owners/due dates. P0.

rows.prd_item: MTG-5: update project/person/decision/daily notes through KnowledgeWriter
MTG-6: route task proposals to Todoist/Linear/Asana per workspace/project policy
MTG-7: propose calendar follow-ups; apply low-risk writes automatically only when policy allows
PRJ-1: all projects share a common project model across workspaces
rows.note: shared project model assumed via project registry (Phase 1) + REQ-F-011; no REQ explicitly mandates a single common model

rows.prd_item: PRJ-2: projects have stable IDs, workspace IDs, aliases, status, outcome, owner, task/calendar/source mappings, progress providers
rows.note: object shape lives in DATA_MODEL §10.2; no dedicated REQ-* enumerates the Project field set

rows.prd_item: PRJ-3: technical projects support IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md progress providers
PRJ-4: deterministic progress from providers, not invented model percentages
TASK-1: personal-life/personal-business tasks route to Todoist by default
rows.note: task routing policy not a dedicated REQ; task creation/routing covered by meeting-closeout REQ-F-007

rows.prd_item: TASK-2: technical work tasks route to Linear when configured
rows.note: as above

rows.prd_item: TASK-3: operations/general work tasks route to Asana when configured
rows.note: as above

rows.prd_item: TASK-4: tasks assigned to others/shared systems require approval unless configured otherwise
ING-1: canonical ingestion event (source ID, workspace, content hash, origin, type, tags, sensitivity, routing hints)
ING-2: support Granola/Telegram/file/URL/YouTube/podcast/PDF/OCR/web/GitHub/Drive sources
ING-3: port YouTube/podcast extraction from Obsidian Second Brain into source adapters
ING-4: low-confidence routing stays in ingestion inbox
ING-5: imported content treated as untrusted; cannot instruct agents/tools
ING-6: deduplicate by content hash, external ID, source URI, workflow idempotency key
rows.note: idempotency via REQ-NF-001; content-hash/external-ID dedup implied by REQ-F-010 SourceEnvelope, not an explicit REQ field

rows.prd_item: ING-7: untrusted-content agents run tool-stripped, rejected at job admission if declaring mutating tools
CAL-1: read all configured calendars for availability and conflict detection
CAL-2: private focus blocks/non-shared reminders may be created automatically when policy allows
CAL-3: inviting others/changing/cancelling shared meetings requires approval
NLM-1: NotebookLM API spike (V1.1 candidate, not V1 deliverable)
NLM-2: V1 ships Drive-backed managed-docs path
BRF-1: generate global and workspace-specific daily/weekly/monthly briefs
BRF-2: briefs saved to Markdown and displayed in Mac dashboard
BRF-3: briefs cite/link supporting notes/tasks/events/sources
LIFE-1: single active instance via exclusive lease; second launch read-only/refuses; stale lock recoverable
LIFE-2: durable Temporal schedules with catch-up; missed firings run once-collapsed within max-staleness window
LIFE-3: in-flight workflows resume with exactly-once side effects via write envelope
LIFE-4: connector-unreachable queue/retry inbound + outbound write outbox; surfaced in System Health
LIFE-5: clock-jump correctness via persisted last-run bookkeeping (monotonic)
rows.note: clock-jump/NTP-correction specifics not called out in REQ acceptance signal; subsumed under restart/sleep durability. P1.

rows.prd_item: LIFE-6: wake hooks subscribe to OS sleep/wake/power + network events to trigger catch-up
rows.note: OS power/network-event subscription not explicitly named in a REQ. P1.

rows.prd_item: LIFE-7: record/display per scheduled workflow last successful run and next scheduled run
rows.note: covered by System Health surface (REQ-UX-001); last/next-run fields not enumerated in a REQ. P1.

rows.prd_item: OBS-1: System Health displays connector status, GBrain health, workflow run status, queue depth, failed write-throughs, sync lag, agent cost, approval backlog
rows.note: System Health surface covered; the specific minimum field set is not enumerated in any REQ acceptance signal

rows.prd_item: OBS-2: each failure class surfaces a distinct, linked, persistent System Health item
rows.note: distinct-per-failure-class surfacing + persistence-until-resolved not explicitly required by any REQ; only the surface exists

rows.prd_item: RET-1: configurable retention policy with documented default for assistant-held raw capture (raw audio deleted after synthesis; 30-day prune default)
rows.note: REQ-F-013 covers only RET-2 user-initiated deletion. NO REQ-* covers the default/configurable retention policy and auto-prune of raw payloads. Named in §20.2 DoD. P0.

rows.prd_item: RET-2: user-initiated cross-store deletion purges Markdown/GBrain/event store + tombstones audit
RET-3: automated pruning never deletes human-owned sections or derived semantic notes
rows.note: No REQ-* covers retention-pruning safety constraints (no deletion of human-owned/derived notes). P1.

rows.prd_item: COST-1: every agent job enforces maxRuntimeSeconds and maxCostUsd; exceed cancels, records, surfaces, no partial side effect
rows.note: NO REQ-* covers per-job runtime/cost-cap enforcement. REQ-NF-001 lists generic hardening but not budget caps. Named in §5.4 metric, §20.1 Budget-cap test, §20.2 DoD. P0.

rows.prd_item: COST-2: Agent Runtime Broker applies a configurable DEFAULT cost cap to LLM jobs lacking one
rows.note: No REQ-* covers the default-cap-for-uncapped-LLM-jobs behavior. P1.

rows.prd_item: EVAL-1: versioned evaluation set + repeatable harness computing §5.4 metrics (meeting-closeout, retrieval, leakage corpora)
Third-Party Egress Policy: per-workspace egressPolicy; Employer Work no raw egress until explicit ack; enforced at Tool Gateway/Broker; shown in System Health
DoD: user can run system locally/self-hosted with documented setup
DoD: Mac app shows global and workspace-specific dashboard data
DoD: Claude SDK reference runtime carries critical path; Hermes passes same bounded-job conformance (required)
DoD: GBrain required, configured, providing search/think/graph/health (read-only sufficient)
rows.note: see KN-2 gap re: capability-surface enumeration

rows.prd_item: DoD: Markdown repos open in Obsidian and remain valid after system writes
DoD: Granola meeting closeout works end-to-end (Employer Work branch requires §16.5 egress ack)
DoD: Calendar/Todoist/Linear/Asana writes governed by policy and idempotency
DoD: daily brief and weekly review generated and saved
DoD: approval inbox works from Mac and Telegram
DoD: at least one YouTube/podcast source adapter operational or explicitly deferred with a ticket
DoD: after sleep/restart, missed schedules catch up once; in-flight workflows resume; single active instance
DoD: user-initiated cross-store deletion (RET-2) and default retention policy (RET-1) work
rows.note: RET-2 covered by REQ-F-013; the RET-1 default-retention-policy half of this DoD bullet has NO covering REQ (see RET-1 gap). P0.

rows.prd_item: DoD: every agent job enforces its runtime/cost caps (COST-1)
rows.note: Same as COST-1 gap — no REQ-* covers runtime/cost-cap enforcement. P0.

rows.prd_item: DoD: EVAL-1 evaluation set exists and computes §5.4 statistical metrics
DoD: untrusted-content agents tool-stripped + rejected at admission (ING-7); cross-workspace reads via Visibility Gate (WS-8)

gaps: WS-5 (§9.1, P1): user-approved explicit cross-workspace links (§6.3 Level 3) — no covering REQ. REQ-F-005 covers cross-workspace READS via GCL but not the creation/approval of explicit links.
WS-6 (§9.1, P1): user-selectable workspace presets (Simple/Professional/Founder/Advanced) — no covering REQ. Phase 10 mentions 'sample workspace presets' but no REQ-* binds the feature.

KN-2 (§9.2, P0): GBrain knowledge-engine capability surface (search, think/synthesis, graph, timelines, schema, health, MCP, Minions, dream) — no REQ enumerates the required capabilities. REQ-F-005/REQ-D-001 only establish GBrain presence + derived/rebuildable.
KN-7 (§9.2, P0): human-owned note sections must not be overwritten — no covering REQ, despite a §20.1 'Human-section preservation' acceptance test that has nothing to trace to.
KN-8 (§9.2, P1): assistant-generated sections use explicit start/end markers + stable IDs — no covering REQ (prerequisite mechanism for KN-7).
MTG-4 (§9.4, P0): never infer task owners/due dates when unstated — mark TBD or route for clarification — no covering REQ. A meeting-closeout data-integrity rule with no traceable requirement.

RET-1 (§9.10, P0): configurable retention policy with documented default for assistant-held raw capture (raw audio deleted after synthesis; 30-day prune default) — no covering REQ. REQ-F-013 covers only RET-2 deletion; the RET-1 half of the §20.2 DoD bullet is unbacked.
RET-3 (§9.10, P1): automated retention pruning must never delete human-owned sections or derived semantic notes — no covering REQ.

COST-1 (§9.11, P0): per-job maxRuntimeSeconds/maxCostUsd enforcement (cancel, record, surface, no partial side effect) — no covering REQ. This is a §5.4 success metric, a §20.1 acceptance test, AND a §20.2 DoD bullet, yet REQUIREMENTS.md has no budget-enforcement requirement (REQ-NF-001 lists only generic hardening).
COST-2 (§9.11, P1): Agent Runtime Broker applies a configurable DEFAULT cost cap to LLM jobs lacking one — no covering REQ.
